rawText:
When Vladimir Putin's generals assure him that Russia's economy is "successfully tackling" the strains of war, they are offering a portrait that omits the cracks spreading beneath the surface -- cracks visible in falling oil revenues, a ballooning budget deficit, and an industrial base that, having been conscripted into military production, can no longer sustain the civilian growth on which long-term stability depends.

Western predictions of an outright economic collapse have, admittedly, proved wide of the mark, since Russia avoided a prolonged recession after 2022 and even managed a brief boom fuelled by a surge in military spending worth roughly five percent of GDP -- a fiscal stimulus that created jobs, filled factory order books, and temporarily masked the corrosive effects of the most comprehensive sanctions regime ever imposed on a major economy.

That boom, however, has given way to near-stagnation, with GDP growth slowing to a fraction of a percent, corporate profits weakening, and purchasing-manager surveys indicating contraction for several consecutive months -- a deterioration driven not only by tighter monetary policy, which pushed interest rates into double digits to combat inflation, but also by the government's pivot from fiscal expansion to mild consolidation.

The Kremlin's dependence on hydrocarbon revenues, which once appeared an unassailable pillar of state finance, has become its most conspicuous vulnerability, as American sanctions targeting Rosneft and Lukoil -- firms that together account for half of Russia's crude exports -- have deterred buyers in India and China, slashed shipments by nearly forty percent, and forced Russian oil onto circuitous routes through shadow tankers and obscure intermediaries whose very proliferation attests to the regime's desperation.

Although the Iran war and the de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz handed Putin an unexpected windfall by driving Brent crude back toward one hundred dollars a barrel, analysts have characterized this reprieve as a "sugar high" rather than a cure, because the elevated prices cannot reverse the structural degradation of Russia's energy infrastructure -- an infrastructure battered by Ukrainian drone strikes, starved of Western components, and left with a mere three hundred thousand barrels per day of spare capacity.

Half of Russia's federal budget now flows to the armed forces, the military-industrial complex, domestic security, and debt service -- a proportion that, while it sustains employment in tank factories working overtime, simultaneously forces car producers to cut shifts, compels the government to borrow domestically at inflationary rates, and necessitates tax increases that break the implicit social contract Putin once offered his people.

The paradox at the heart of the war economy is that military production makes Russia busier but poorer, generating few lasting assets or productivity gains, while the labour shortages it creates -- with unemployment at historic lows and real wages at nominal highs -- conceal a reality in which workers are being siphoned from productive sectors into an enterprise whose output is, by definition, consumed in destruction.

Public sentiment, long treated as irrelevant in a dictatorship, has begun to shift in ways the Kremlin's own pollsters cannot ignore: surveys reveal that the share of Russians reporting deteriorating well-being has tripled relative to those claiming improvement, that a majority now perceives opposition to the war as more socially acceptable than enthusiasm, and that veterans are increasingly viewed not as heroes but as victims or threats.

Even if peace were to arrive tomorrow, Russia would face a grim economic reckoning, because an economy restructured around military output cannot simply revert to civilian production without triggering unemployment among demobilized soldiers, excess capacity in munitions factories, and a fiscal crisis born of revenues that no longer match inflated wartime spending -- a dilemma that gives Putin, paradoxically, an incentive to prolong the very conflict that is hollowing out his state.

The question, therefore, is not whether Russia's economy will collapse in a single dramatic convulsion but whether the slow, grinding erosion of its productive capacity, its fiscal buffers, and its citizens' tolerance will eventually compel a recalculation in the Kremlin -- or whether Putin, lacking both an exit strategy and the political space to pursue one, will continue to double down on repression and rearmament until the illusion of resilience becomes impossible even for state television to sustain.

overview.title: Russia's War Economy: Resilience or Illusion?

sentences.original: When Vladimir Putin's generals assure him that Russia's economy is "successfully tackling" the strains of war, they are offering a portrait that omits the cracks spreading beneath the surface -- cracks visible in falling oil revenues, a ballooning budget deficit, and an industrial base that, having been conscripted into military production, can no longer sustain the civilian growth on which long-term stability depends.
sentences.translation: 푸틴의 장군들이 러시아 경제가 전쟁의 부담을 "성공적으로 극복하고 있다"고 보고할 때, 그들은 표면 아래로 퍼져 나가는 균열을 빠뜨린 초상화를 바치고 있는 것이다 -- 하락하는 석유 수입, 급팽창하는 재정 적자, 그리고 군수 생산에 동원되어 더 이상 장기적 안정의 토대인 민간 성장을 지탱할 수 없게 된 산업 기반에서 드러나는 균열 말이다.

grammarElements.text: When Vladimir Putin's generals assure him that ...
grammarElements.label: 시간 종속절

grammarElements.text: they are offering
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 주절 동사

grammarElements.text: a portrait that omits the cracks
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

grammarElements.text: having been conscripted into military production
grammarElements.label: 완료 분사구문 (수동)

grammarElements.text: on which long-term stability depends
grammarElements.label: 전치사 관계절

vocabulary.example: The ballooning deficit forced the government to reconsider its spending plans.
Factories were effectively conscripted into producing military equipment.

expressions.usage: 예측이나 평가가 실제 결과와 크게 달랐을 때 사용하는 관용 표현

patterns.explanation: 어떤 주장이 이루어지는 상황과 그 주장이 실제로 의미하는 바를 대조하는 패턴
examples: When officials claim that growth is strong, they are painting a picture that ignores the underlying data.
When leaders promise that reforms will work, they are offering assurances that overlook key risks.

drillQuestions.question: They are offering a portrait that ______ the cracks spreading beneath the surface.

sentences.notes: 기사 전체의 핵심 프레임을 설정하는 도입문. 분사구문과 전치사 관계절이 중첩되어 문장이 길지만, 핵심은 '장군들의 보고 vs 실제 균열'이라는 대조 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: a portrait that omits the cracks

sentences.original: Western predictions of an outright economic collapse have, admittedly, proved wide of the mark, since Russia avoided a prolonged recession after 2022 and even managed a brief boom fuelled by a surge in military spending worth roughly five percent of GDP -- a fiscal stimulus that created jobs, filled factory order books, and temporarily masked the corrosive effects of the most comprehensive sanctions regime ever imposed on a major economy.
sentences.translation: 서방의 전면적 경제 붕괴 예측은, 인정하건대, 빗나간 것으로 드러났다. 러시아가 2022년 이후 장기 침체를 피했고 GDP의 약 5%에 달하는 군비 지출 급증에 힘입어 짧은 호황까지 만들어냈기 때문이다 -- 일자리를 창출하고, 공장 수주를 채우고, 주요 경제국에 부과된 역사상 가장 포괄적인 제재 체제의 부식 효과를 일시적으로 가린 재정 부양책이었다.

grammarElements.text: Western predictions of an outright economic collapse
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: have, admittedly, proved
grammarElements.label: 삽입 부사를 포함한 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: wide of the mark
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관용 표현)

grammarElements.text: since Russia avoided ... and even managed ...
grammarElements.label: since 이유절

vocabulary.example: The corrosive effects of inflation eroded household savings over the decade.

expressions.usage: 예측이나 추정이 현실과 크게 어긋났을 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 예측이 틀렸음을 인정하면서 그 이유를 제시하는 양보 구조
examples: Early forecasts proved overly pessimistic, since the recovery came faster than expected.
The warning proved premature, since demand held up through the quarter.

drillQuestions.question: Western predictions of an outright economic collapse have proved wide of the ______.

sentences.notes: admittedly라는 삽입 부사가 논쟁적 주제에서 상대 주장을 먼저 인정한 뒤 반박하는 수사적 전략을 보여준다.

sentences.original: That boom, however, has given way to near-stagnation, with GDP growth slowing to a fraction of a percent, corporate profits weakening, and purchasing-manager surveys indicating contraction for several consecutive months -- a deterioration driven not only by tighter monetary policy, which pushed interest rates into double digits to combat inflation, but also by the government's pivot from fiscal expansion to mild consolidation.
sentences.translation: 하지만 그 호황은 거의 정체 상태로 전환되었다. GDP 성장률이 소수점 이하로 둔화되고, 기업 이익이 약화되며, 구매관리자 지수가 여러 달 연속 수축을 가리키고 있다 -- 이 악화는 인플레이션 억제를 위해 금리를 두 자릿수로 끌어올린 긴축 통화 정책뿐 아니라, 정부가 재정 확장에서 온건한 긴축으로 전환한 데서도 비롯되었다.

grammarElements.text: That boom
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has given way to
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 구동사

grammarElements.text: near-stagnation
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: with GDP growth slowing ..., corporate profits weakening ..., and ... surveys indicating ...
grammarElements.label: with + 독립분사구문 (병렬)

grammarElements.text: not only by ... but also by ...
grammarElements.label: 상관접속사 구조

vocabulary.example: Years of stagnation left the economy vulnerable to external shocks.
Fiscal consolidation often means spending cuts and higher taxes.

expressions.usage: 한 상태가 다른 상태로 바뀔 때 쓰는 표현으로, 보통 긍정에서 부정으로의 전환에 많이 사용된다

patterns.pattern: [Phase A] has given way to [Phase B], with [indicator 1], [indicator 2], and [indicator 3]
patterns.explanation: 한 국면이 다른 국면으로 전환되었음을 구체적 지표들과 함께 보여주는 패턴
examples: The initial optimism has given way to caution, with sales declining, inventories rising, and hiring slowing.
The boom has given way to a downturn, with exports falling and consumer confidence dropping.

drillQuestions.question: That boom has given ______ to near-stagnation.

sentences.notes: with + 분사구문의 병렬 구조가 경제 악화의 복합적 증거를 나열하며, not only ... but also가 원인의 이중성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: pivot from fiscal expansion to mild consolidation

sentences.original: The Kremlin's dependence on hydrocarbon revenues, which once appeared an unassailable pillar of state finance, has become its most conspicuous vulnerability, as American sanctions targeting Rosneft and Lukoil -- firms that together account for half of Russia's crude exports -- have deterred buyers in India and China, slashed shipments by nearly forty percent, and forced Russian oil onto circuitous routes through shadow tankers and obscure intermediaries whose very proliferation attests to the regime's desperation.
sentences.translation: 한때 국가 재정의 난공불락의 기둥처럼 보였던 탄화수소 수입에 대한 크렘린의 의존은 가장 두드러진 취약점이 되었다. 러시아 원유 수출의 절반을 차지하는 로스네프트와 루코일을 겨냥한 미국의 제재가 인도와 중국의 구매자들을 위축시키고, 선적량을 거의 40% 삭감했으며, 러시아 원유를 그림자 유조선과 무명의 중개업체를 경유하는 우회 경로로 내몰았기 때문이다 -- 그 중개업체의 급증 자체가 정권의 절박함을 입증한다.

grammarElements.text: The Kremlin's dependence on hydrocarbon revenues
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which once appeared an unassailable pillar
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

grammarElements.text: has become
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: its most conspicuous vulnerability
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: as American sanctions ... have deterred ..., slashed ..., and forced ...
grammarElements.label: as 이유절 + 동사 병렬

vocabulary.example: The company's conspicuous absence from the trade fair raised questions.
Sanctions forced exports onto circuitous routes that added cost and delay.

expressions.usage: 비율이나 몫을 설명할 때 빈번히 쓰이는 핵심 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject]'s dependence on [X] has become its [greatest] vulnerability, as [cause]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 강점이 현재의 약점으로 전환되었음을 인과 관계와 함께 설명하는 패턴
examples: The firm's dependence on a single client has become its greatest risk, as that client now faces bankruptcy.
The nation's reliance on imported chips has become a strategic weakness, as supply chains have fractured.

drillQuestions.question: The Kremlin's dependence on hydrocarbon revenues has become its most ______ vulnerability.

sentences.notes: 강점이 약점으로 반전되는 아이러니를 긴 문장 속에 담았다. whose very proliferation attests to 구문은 부연이면서 동시에 핵심 논거 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: an unassailable pillar of state finance

sentences.original: Although the Iran war and the de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz handed Putin an unexpected windfall by driving Brent crude back toward one hundred dollars a barrel, analysts have characterized this reprieve as a "sugar high" rather than a cure, because the elevated prices cannot reverse the structural degradation of Russia's energy infrastructure -- an infrastructure battered by Ukrainian drone strikes, starved of Western components, and left with a mere three hundred thousand barrels per day of spare capacity.
sentences.translation: 이란 전쟁과 호르무즈 해협의 사실상 폐쇄가 브렌트유를 배럴당 100달러 선으로 끌어올려 푸틴에게 예상치 못한 횡재를 안겨주었지만, 분석가들은 이 유예를 치료가 아닌 "일시적 흥분"으로 규정했다. 고유가가 러시아 에너지 인프라의 구조적 열화를 되돌릴 수 없기 때문이다 -- 우크라이나 드론 공격에 타격을 입고, 서방 부품이 차단되어, 하루 예비 생산량이 겨우 30만 배럴에 불과한 인프라 말이다.

grammarElements.text: Although the Iran war ... handed Putin an unexpected windfall
grammarElements.label: 양보절

grammarElements.text: analysts have characterized
grammarElements.label: 주절 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: this reprieve as a "sugar high" rather than a cure
grammarElements.label: characterize A as B 구문

grammarElements.text: because the elevated prices cannot reverse ...
grammarElements.label: because 이유절

vocabulary.example: The ceasefire offered only a temporary reprieve from the bombardment.

expressions.usage: 효과가 오래가지 않는 일시적 자극을 비유적으로 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Although [positive event], [subject] has characterized [it] as [negative assessment] rather than [positive assessment]
patterns.explanation: 겉보기에 긍정적인 사건의 본질이 부정적임을 전문가 평가와 함께 밝히는 패턴
examples: Although sales rose sharply, analysts characterized the jump as a one-off rather than a trend.
Although the truce held, observers characterized it as a pause rather than a peace.

drillQuestions.question: Analysts have characterized this reprieve as a "sugar high" rather than a ______.

sentences.notes: Although + 양보, rather than + 대조, because + 이유라는 세 겹의 논리 구조가 '유가 상승도 해결책이 아니다'라는 핵심 주장을 단단히 뒷받침한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a sugar high rather than a cure

sentences.original: Half of Russia's federal budget now flows to the armed forces, the military-industrial complex, domestic security, and debt service -- a proportion that, while it sustains employment in tank factories working overtime, simultaneously forces car producers to cut shifts, compels the government to borrow domestically at inflationary rates, and necessitates tax increases that break the implicit social contract Putin once offered his people.
sentences.translation: 러시아 연방 예산의 절반이 현재 군대, 군산복합체, 국내 안보, 그리고 채무 상환에 흘러 들어간다 -- 이 비율은 초과 근무 중인 전차 공장의 고용을 유지하는 동시에, 자동차 생산업체에게는 교대 근무를 줄이도록 강제하고, 정부에게는 인플레이션을 부추기는 금리로 국내 차입을 하도록 압박하며, 푸틴이 한때 국민에게 제시했던 암묵적 사회 계약을 깨뜨리는 세금 인상을 불가피하게 만든다.

grammarElements.text: Half of Russia's federal budget
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: now flows to
grammarElements.label: 현재시제 동사

grammarElements.text: the armed forces, the military-industrial complex, domestic security, and debt service
grammarElements.label: 병렬 목적어

grammarElements.text: while it sustains ..., simultaneously forces ..., compels ..., and necessitates ...
grammarElements.label: 양보절 + 병렬 동사구

vocabulary.example: The crisis necessitated emergency measures that no one had planned for.

expressions.usage: 정부와 국민 사이의 명시되지 않은 상호 약속을 가리키는 정치학적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Proportion] flows to [A, B, C] -- a proportion that, while it [positive effect], simultaneously [negative effect 1], [negative effect 2], and [negative effect 3]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 정책이 긍정적 효과와 부정적 효과를 동시에 만들어내는 이중성을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Most of the aid flows to the military -- a share that, while it boosts morale, simultaneously drains funds from schools and hospitals.
A third of revenue flows to debt payments -- a burden that, while it preserves creditworthiness, simultaneously starves investment.

drillQuestions.question: This proportion ______ tax increases that break the implicit social contract.

sentences.notes: while과 simultaneously의 대조가 군비 지출의 양면성 -- 고용 유지와 경제 왜곡 -- 을 한 문장 안에서 선명하게 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: forces car producers to cut shifts
koreanAlignment.ko: 자동차 생산업체에게 교대 근무를 줄이도록 강제하다

sentences.original: The paradox at the heart of the war economy is that military production makes Russia busier but poorer, generating few lasting assets or productivity gains, while the labour shortages it creates -- with unemployment at historic lows and real wages at nominal highs -- conceal a reality in which workers are being siphoned from productive sectors into an enterprise whose output is, by definition, consumed in destruction.
sentences.translation: 전시경제의 핵심에 있는 역설은 군수 생산이 러시아를 더 바쁘게 만들되 더 가난하게 만든다는 것이다. 지속적인 자산이나 생산성 향상을 거의 만들어내지 못하는 반면, 군수 생산이 초래한 노동력 부족 -- 실업률은 역대 최저, 실질 임금은 명목상 최고 -- 은 노동자들이 생산적 부문에서 그 산출물이 정의상 파괴 속에서 소비되는 사업으로 빨려 들어가고 있는 현실을 감추고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The paradox at the heart of the war economy
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is that
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 명사절 접속사

grammarElements.text: military production makes Russia busier but poorer
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (주절 보어)

grammarElements.text: while the labour shortages it creates ... conceal a reality
grammarElements.label: while 대조절

vocabulary.example: Talent is being siphoned away from research into more lucrative fields.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것의 본질에서 필연적으로 따라오는 결론임을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The paradox is that [A makes X better] but [poorer], while [apparent positive] conceals [hidden negative]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 지표의 긍정과 내면적 실체의 부정을 동시에 드러내는 역설 구조
examples: The paradox is that cheap credit makes consumers richer on paper but more indebted in reality.
The paradox is that automation makes factories more efficient while concealing the displacement of workers.

drillQuestions.question: Workers are being ______ from productive sectors into an enterprise whose output is consumed in destruction.

sentences.notes: busier but poorer라는 간결한 대조가 전시경제의 본질적 모순을 포착하며, by definition이 군수 생산의 비생산성을 논리적으로 못 박는다.

koreanAlignment.en: The paradox at the heart of

sentences.original: Public sentiment, long treated as irrelevant in a dictatorship, has begun to shift in ways the Kremlin's own pollsters cannot ignore: surveys reveal that the share of Russians reporting deteriorating well-being has tripled relative to those claiming improvement, that a majority now perceives opposition to the war as more socially acceptable than enthusiasm, and that veterans are increasingly viewed not as heroes but as victims or threats.
sentences.translation: 독재 체제에서 오랫동안 무관하다고 취급받아 온 여론이 크렘린 자체 여론조사원들도 무시할 수 없는 방식으로 변하기 시작했다. 조사에 따르면 삶이 나빠지고 있다고 보고하는 러시아인의 비율이 나아졌다는 사람에 비해 세 배로 늘었고, 다수가 이제 전쟁에 대한 반대를 열성보다 사회적으로 더 수용 가능하게 인식하며, 참전 용사들이 점점 더 영웅이 아닌 피해자나 위협으로 여겨지고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Public sentiment
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: long treated as irrelevant in a dictatorship
grammarElements.label: 축약된 과거분사 수식어

grammarElements.text: has begun to shift
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: surveys reveal that ..., that ..., and that ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 세 개의 that절 병렬

vocabulary.example: Deteriorating infrastructure has made daily commutes increasingly difficult.

expressions.usage: 비교 대상을 명시할 때 쓰는 격식 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject], long treated as [irrelevant], has begun to [shift] in ways [authority] cannot ignore
patterns.explanation: 오래 무시되어 온 요소가 마침내 변화하여 권위자도 무시할 수 없게 되었음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Consumer anger, long dismissed as noise, has begun to affect sales in ways executives cannot ignore.
Environmental damage, long treated as external, has begun to hit profits in ways boards cannot overlook.

drillQuestions.question: The share of Russians reporting deteriorating well-being has ______ relative to those claiming improvement.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤에 세 개의 that절이 병렬로 이어지는 고급 구조. 각 that절이 여론 변화의 서로 다른 측면을 보여주어 근거의 층위를 쌓는다.

koreanAlignment.en: Public sentiment, long treated as irrelevant
not as heroes but as victims or threats

pronunciationNotes: deteriorating [dɪˈtɪəriəreɪtɪŋ] -- 다섯 음절, 두 번째 음절 강세

sentences.original: Even if peace were to arrive tomorrow, Russia would face a grim economic reckoning, because an economy restructured around military output cannot simply revert to civilian production without triggering unemployment among demobilized soldiers, excess capacity in munitions factories, and a fiscal crisis born of revenues that no longer match inflated wartime spending -- a dilemma that gives Putin, paradoxically, an incentive to prolong the very conflict that is hollowing out his state.
sentences.translation: 설령 내일 당장 평화가 찾아온다 해도 러시아는 암울한 경제적 청산에 직면할 것이다. 군수 생산 중심으로 재편된 경제는 제대 군인 사이의 실업, 군수 공장의 과잉 설비, 그리고 부풀려진 전시 지출에 더 이상 부합하지 않는 세수에서 비롯된 재정 위기를 유발하지 않고서는 민간 생산으로 복귀할 수 없기 때문이다 -- 역설적이게도 이 딜레마가 푸틴에게 자신의 국가를 안에서부터 비워내고 있는 바로 그 전쟁을 연장할 동기를 부여한다.

grammarElements.text: Even if peace were to arrive tomorrow
grammarElements.label: 가정법 과거 조건절

grammarElements.text: Russia would face
grammarElements.label: 가정법 주절 동사

grammarElements.text: a grim economic reckoning
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: because an economy ... cannot simply revert to ... without triggering ...
grammarElements.label: 이유절 + without + 동명사

grammarElements.text: that gives Putin ... an incentive to prolong
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: Years of fiscal mismanagement led to a painful economic reckoning.

expressions.usage: 외형은 유지되나 내부 실질이 사라지는 상황을 비유적으로 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Even if [best case], [subject] would face [consequence], because [structural reason]
patterns.explanation: 최선의 시나리오에서도 문제가 해결되지 않음을 보여주는 가정법 구조
examples: Even if demand recovered tomorrow, the company would face layoffs, because its cost structure has changed permanently.
Even if oil prices doubled, the deficit would remain, because spending has outpaced any plausible revenue scenario.

drillQuestions.question: Even if peace were to arrive tomorrow, Russia would face a grim economic ______.

sentences.notes: 가정법 과거(were to)가 '평화가 와도'라는 비현실적 상황을 설정하고, 그조차 해결책이 아님을 논증하는 강력한 수사 전략이다. hollowing out이 국가의 내부 공동화를 생생하게 그린다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even if peace were to arrive tomorrow
koreanAlignment.ko: 설령 내일 당장 평화가 찾아온다 해도

koreanAlignment.en: cannot simply revert to civilian production
koreanAlignment.ko: 민간 생산으로 간단히 복귀할 수 없다

pronunciationNotes: reckoning [ˈrekənɪŋ] -- '레커닝'으로 첫 음절 강세, 두 음절

sentences.original: The question, therefore, is not whether Russia's economy will collapse in a single dramatic convulsion but whether the slow, grinding erosion of its productive capacity, its fiscal buffers, and its citizens' tolerance will eventually compel a recalculation in the Kremlin -- or whether Putin, lacking both an exit strategy and the political space to pursue one, will continue to double down on repression and rearmament until the illusion of resilience becomes impossible even for state television to sustain.
sentences.translation: 따라서 문제는 러시아 경제가 한 번의 극적인 경련으로 무너질 것인가가 아니라, 생산 능력, 재정 완충 장치, 그리고 시민들의 인내에 대한 느리고 갈아내는 침식이 결국 크렘린 내부의 재계산을 강제할 것인가 -- 아니면 출구 전략도 그것을 추구할 정치적 공간도 없는 푸틴이 회복력의 환상을 국영 방송조차 유지할 수 없게 될 때까지 탄압과 재무장에 계속 배팅을 높여갈 것인가이다.

grammarElements.text: The question
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is not whether ... but whether ...
grammarElements.label: not A but B 구조

grammarElements.text: the slow, grinding erosion of ...
grammarElements.label: 내포절의 주어

grammarElements.text: or whether Putin ... will continue to double down
grammarElements.label: 세 번째 선택지

grammarElements.text: lacking both an exit strategy and the political space
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 부대상황

vocabulary.example: The financial convulsion of 2008 reshaped global banking.
The erosion of public trust made reform nearly impossible.

expressions.usage: 실패 가능성에도 기존 전략을 더 강화할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: The question is not whether [dramatic scenario] but whether [gradual scenario] -- or whether [worst-case continuation]
patterns.explanation: 문제의 진짜 본질이 극적 사건이 아닌 점진적 과정에 있음을 재설정하는 결론부 패턴
examples: The question is not whether the firm will go bankrupt overnight but whether the steady loss of market share will force a sale.
The question is not whether democracy will fall in a coup but whether the slow erosion of norms will render elections meaningless.

drillQuestions.question: Putin will continue to double ______ on repression and rearmament.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마무리 문장으로, not whether A but whether B -- or whether C라는 삼중 구조가 러시아 경제의 미래를 세 가지 시나리오로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: double down on repression and rearmament

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: rhetorical question / tripartite structure (수사적 질문 / 삼분 구조)
